summary: Context scan for password manager support on the native login form.

findings.evidence: screens/LoginScreen.tsx renders username, password, captcha, and 2FA fields with StyledTextInput inside react-hook-form FormControl components.
Before the fix, username had autoComplete="username" and password had secureTextEntry plus autoComplete="current-password". No explicit textContentType or importantForAutofill was set.
components/StyledTextInput.tsx forwards ...props to React Native TextInput, so autofill-related props are not swallowed by the wrapper.
React Native 0.81.5 maps current-password to Android password autofill hints and iOS textContentType=password, and maps username to platform username hints.
servicies/auth.ts fetches /signin, parses V2EX dynamic username/password/captcha field names, then posts form-urlencoded data. The native form is not a browser DOM form.
app.json has no Associated Domains webcredentials entitlement and no Android Digital Asset Links or asset_statements configuration in the repository.

risks: Native TextInput hints improve detection but do not guarantee that every third-party password manager will offer saved website credentials.
Full matching to www.v2ex.com credentials requires platform-level app-site association outside this local code-only change.
No mobile device autofill run was performed in this turn; validation is static.
